skill.name: pydantic-models-py
skill.description: Create Pydantic v2 model sets for Python APIs using Base, Create, Update, Response, and InDB variants.
skill.summary: Generate consistent Pydantic v2 schemas for API contracts and database documents.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported external command, network, and weak cryptography patterns, but review found these are false positives in Markdown and Pydantic examples. No prompt injection, data exfiltration, command execution, or malicious intent was found in SKILL.md. The skill is safe to publish with low residual risk from being community-authored documentation.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backtick Static Matches Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The external command findings are false positives. The reported lines contain Markdown inline code, tables, and Python example blocks for Pydantic models, not shell execution instructions. Confidence: 0.96. Confidence reasoning: The reviewed locations show documentation and static examples only, with no command runner, subprocess call, shell invocation, or user-controlled execution path.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed locations show Markdown formatting and Python class examples only. No executable shell commands or external process APIs are present.

low_findings.title: Network Finding Is A PATCH Text False Positive
low_findings.description: The network finding is a false positive. Line 41 describes fields for PATCH requests inside a Python docstring, and no HTTP library, URL, request call, or data transfer is present. Confidence: 0.95. Confidence reasoning: The evidence is a plain documentation sentence in a model example, not network-capable code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The evidence is a docstring about PATCH request semantics. No network API usage or endpoint appears in the skill file.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Matches Are Text False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptographic algorithm findings are false positives. The reported lines use the word description in front matter and an example field name, and they do not define or invoke DES, MD5, SHA1, or any cryptographic operation. Confidence: 0.94. Confidence reasoning: The reviewed lines are metadata and schema field examples, with no cryptographic imports, parameters, hashes, encryption, or security-sensitive processing.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The evidence is ordinary prose and a field named description. No cryptographic code or algorithm selection exists.

content.value_statement: Python API teams need consistent request, response, and database schemas. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to create Pydantic v2 models with a repeatable multi-model pattern.
actual_capabilities: Creates Base, Create, Update, Response, and InDB model variants.
Applies Pydantic v2 Field definitions and validation constraints.
Uses camelCase aliases while allowing snake_case field population.
Models optional update fields for PATCH-style request bodies.
Adds database document fields such as doc_type for stored records.
Suggests model placement and export steps for backend projects.
limitations: It is guidance documentation, not an executable model generator.
It does not inspect an existing database schema automatically.
It assumes Python projects that already use Pydantic v2 patterns.
It does not create matching TypeScript types by itself.

use_cases.title: Define API Contracts
use_cases.description: Create consistent request and response schemas for a new Python API resource.

use_cases.title: Standardize Validation Models
use_cases.description: Convert informal field lists into structured Pydantic models with validation rules.

use_cases.title: Prepare Database Documents
use_cases.description: Add stored-record model variants that include response fields and document metadata.

prompt_templates.title: Create Basic Models
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner model creation for a simple resource.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create Pydantic v2 models for a Project resource with name, description, workspace_id, created_at, and updated_at fields.

prompt_templates.title: Add Validation Rules
prompt_templates.scenario: Add field constraints for API input safety.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create Base, Create, Update, Response, and InDB models for a Task resource. Include validation for title length, optional notes, and status values.

prompt_templates.title: Align API And Database Models
prompt_templates.scenario: Model request bodies, responses, and stored documents together.
prompt_templates.prompt: Design Pydantic v2 models for an Invoice resource. Use camelCase aliases, optional update fields, response timestamps, and an InDB doc_type value.

prompt_templates.title: Refactor Existing Schemas
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced cleanup of inconsistent model definitions.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these existing Pydantic models and refactor them into the Base, Create, Update, Response, and InDB pattern while preserving API compatibility.

output_examples.input: Request models for a Project resource with name and workspace identifier.
output: A shared base model contains common project fields.
A create model requires fields needed for new projects.
An update model makes editable fields optional.
A response model includes identifiers and timestamps.

output_examples.input: Database model for a stored project document.
output_examples.output: The response model is extended with a document type field so database queries can distinguish the stored resource.

output_examples.input: Models that accept camelCase API payloads and snake_case Python usage.
output_examples.output: Fields receive camelCase aliases while model configuration allows population by the Python field names.

best_practices: Start with the shared Base model before adding request or response variants.
Keep update models optional so partial changes do not require unchanged fields.
Use aliases consistently when API payloads use camelCase and Python code uses snake_case.
anti_patterns: Do not reuse database models directly as public API response models.
Do not make every update field required for partial update endpoints.
Do not mix unrelated resource fields into one shared base model.

faq.question: Does this skill generate executable files?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance for creating Pydantic model code in your project.

faq.question: Which Pydantic version does it target?
faq.answer: It targets Pydantic v2 patterns and field definitions.

faq.question: Can it support FastAPI projects?
faq.answer: Yes. The model pattern fits common FastAPI request and response schemas.

faq.question: Why create separate Create and Update models?
faq.answer: Create models require fields for new records, while Update models allow partial changes.

faq.question: Does it handle camelCase JSON fields?
faq.answer: Yes. The guidance shows how to define aliases for camelCase API payloads.

faq.question: Does it include database-specific models?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes an InDB variant for stored documents with document metadata.
